seoTitle: Sync Monday.com with Outlook Calendar (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Connect Monday.com to Outlook Calendar so task deadlines and milestones appear automatically, avoiding manual double-booking for your team.
intro: For teams running on Microsoft 365, keeping Monday.com boards and Outlook Calendar in sync manually leads to missed events and duplicate entries. This guide shows how to connect the two using Monday.com's integration center, choose a sync direction, and map board data into Outlook's calendar fields so deadlines and milestones show up automatically.

steps.heading: Open the integration center
steps.body: From your master board, open the integration feature dashboard and find the official Outlook integration card.

steps.heading: Pick your sync direction
steps.body: Choose between a one-way sync (Monday to Outlook) or a live two-way connection depending on how your team manages events.

steps.heading: Authenticate your Microsoft account
steps.body: Sign in with your Microsoft 365 or Outlook account and grant calendar write permissions.

steps.heading: Map columns to Outlook fields
steps.body: Match Task Name, Date, Timeline, and People columns to Outlook's Subject, Start/End Time, and Description fields.

steps.heading: Set conditional sync triggers
steps.body: Limit which items sync by criteria such as status changing to "Working on it" or a specific team member being assigned.

steps.heading: Run a live test
steps.body: Update a board item and confirm the event appears instantly in Outlook, checking time zone accuracy for recurring items.

faq.question: Does this work with Microsoft 365 business accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, the integration authenticates through your Microsoft 365 or Outlook account and syncs to your corporate calendar.

faq.question: Can I sync only tasks assigned to me?
faq.answer: Yes, conditional triggers let you filter which items sync based on assignment or status changes.

faq.question: Is the sync one-way or two-way?
faq.answer: You can choose either — a one-way sync pushes from Monday.com to Outlook, while a two-way connection keeps both live.

faq.question: Will recurring tasks cause duplicate events?
faq.answer: The integration includes best practices for managing recurring scheduling shifts, but it's worth reviewing your rules after the first sync to confirm there's no clutter.
